title: Sour Flaky Ethiopian Tofu Stew
description: A bright, tangy stew of flaky tofu and aromatic berbere, finished with a hint of parmesan for a creamy finish. Perfect for a light, diabetic-friendly dinner that feels like a culinary adventure.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in minced garlic and ginger, cooking until fragrant, 1 minute.
Add berbere and tomato paste, mix well and cook for 2 minutes to bloom the spices.
Pour in vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Fold in tofu flakes gently, allowing them to absorb the flavors for 5 minutes.
Add lemon juice, salt, and pepper, adjusting seasoning to taste.
Simmer the stew for an additional 3 minutes so the tofu crisps slightly on the edges.
Stir in grated parmesan, allowing it to melt into a silky coating on the tofu.
Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of fresh herbs if desired.
